'''Warren Jowitt''' (born 9 September 1974), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Woz"''', is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s, Jowitt was known for his hard running and uncompromising defence and was a no-nonsense kind of player coached in the 2000s and 2010s.

==Background==
Warren Jowitt was born in [[Wakefield]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

His son Billy Jowitt is a rugby league footballer who plays for [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>[https://hunsletrlfc.com/player/billy-jowitt/ Billy Jowitt]</ref>

==Playing career==
He played at club level for the [[Stanley Rangers]] ARLFC, [[Hunslet RLFC|Hunslet Hawks]], the [[Bradford Bulls]], the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Hull FC]], and the [[Dewsbury Rams]], as a {{rlp|pr|sr}}, or {{rlp|lf}},<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

===Financial crisis at the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
In 2000, at the height of a financial crisis at the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats, the contracts of all players aged over 24 were terminated during September 2000. The players affected were; [[Andy Fisher (rugby league)|Andy Fisher]], [[Bobbie Goulding]], Warren Jowitt, [[Tony Kemp (rugby league)|Tony Kemp]] (player-coach), [[Steve McNamara]], [[Francis Maloney (rugby league)|Francis Maloney]], [[Martin Masella]], [[Steve Prescott]], [[Bright Sodje]], [[Francis Stephenson]], and [[Glen Tomlinson]].<ref name="Goodway calls for aid package">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1295270.stm|title=Goodway calls for aid package|publisher=BBC|date=24 April 2001|access-date=1 January 2012}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
He has coached at club level for [[Dewsbury Rams]], and [[France national rugby league team|France]] (assistant)<ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and only the second coach in the game to achieve an undefeated season.{{citation needed|date=June 2026}}
